Went to OMJ website  
communicating the brand I want to project — premium.

**1\. Hero Section (Needs a Complete Redesign)**

The first thing I see is people holding juice.

I actually **don’t care about the people.**

I want to see:

🍊 Oranges.

Juice.

The machine squeezing fresh oranges.

The juice pouring into the cup.

Imagine opening [Apple.com](http://Apple.com/)—you see the product immediately.

For OMJ, your product is the fresh juice experience.

Instead of:

Manila’s Best Freshly-Squezed Orange Juice

Use:

**Freshly Squeezed. Every Cup. Every Time.**

Subheadline:

Made from whole oranges.

No sugar.  
No water.  
No concentrates.

Then a video autoplaying of the machine squeezing oranges.

⸻

**2\. The Machine is Hidden**

This surprised me.

The vending machine is your competitive advantage, yet it only appears near the bottom of the page.

Move it much higher.

Show:

Machine  
Oranges inside  
Squeezing process  
Finished cup

People are fascinated by seeing the process.

⸻

**3\. Too Much Reading**

There are large paragraphs.

Most visitors won’t read them.

Instead of:

“Our founder traveled around the world…”

Break it down into sections:

**Why OMJ**

Real oranges.

Nothing artificial.

Freshly squeezed in under a minute.

Short, visual, and easy to scan.

⸻

**4\. Replace Checkmarks with Premium Icons**

The green checkmarks look generic.

Instead, use clean icons:

🍊 100% Fresh Oranges

💧 No Water Added

🚫 No Added Sugar

❄️ Served Fresh

⚡ Ready in Under 60 Seconds

This small change makes the site feel more premium.

⸻

**5\. Show the Product Much More**

I only saw one bottle and one cup.

I want:

Cold juice with condensation  
Ice  
Whole oranges  
Close-up of the vibrant juice  
Someone enjoying the drink

The product should be the hero.

⸻

**6\. Add a “Find a Machine” Section**

This should be prominent.

📍 Greenhills

📍 Eastwood

📍 Uptown

📍 Paseo

📍 Parqal

With buttons:

Directions

Open in Maps

⸻

**7\. Remove the “Leave a Reply” Section**

This is a blog comment form and doesn’t belong on a brand website.

It makes the site look unfinished.

Replace it with:

“Questions?”

Contact Us

⸻

**8\. Franchise Section**

Instead of:

Interested to Franchise?

Use:

**Own an OMJ Machine**

Subheadline:

Join the fastest-growing fresh juice concept in the Philippines.

Button:

Join the Waitlist

If you’re not actively franchising yet, this still captures interest without overcommitting.

⸻

**9\. Add Numbers**

People trust measurable facts.

Examples:

🍊 Over 500,000 oranges squeezed  
🥤 Thousands of cups served  
📍 Multiple Metro Manila locations  
⭐ 100% fresh juice

Update these as your business grows.

⸻

**10\. End With a Strong CTA**

Finish with:

**Ready for Real Orange Juice?**

Find the nearest OMJ machine today.

\[Find a Location\]

\[Order Online\]

⸻

**The Bigger Opportunity**

Because you’re also planning **Fruitty Dippy**, I think OMJ should evolve into a broader **fresh fruit brand**, not just a vending machine company.

That opens the door to:

Fresh orange juice  
Chocolate-dipped fruit (Fruitty Dippy)  
Fresh fruit cups  
Seasonal fruit offerings  
Corporate events and catering

One note: if that’s your long-term direction, avoid making the website look like it’s only about vending machines. Instead, make it about **fresh fruit experiences**, with the vending machines being one way customers access the brand.

If I were rebuilding OMJ from scratch today, I’d model the user experience after premium consumer brands like Apple or high-end food and beverage companies: minimal text, exceptional photography, short videos, bold typography, and a clear focus on the product. The goal would be for visitors to immediately think, **“I want to try that,”** within the first five seconds.

Here’s the new cup design.![][image1]  
![][image2]  
Change to actual machine please?

![][image3]  
Not people. Oranges  
![][image4]  
We don’t offer in bottles  
![][image5]  
1 line only for  
No added preservatives

And not “no added  
Preservatives”

And

Remove na:  
Delivered about the metro

We don’t deliver

I remember we discussed removing “order now “ na?
